data.title: What is the White House Correspondents' Dinner? — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

The White House Correspondents' Dinner is an annual black-tie gala event hosted in Washington D.C. by the White House Correspondents Association. The thousands of guests are a mix of journalists, government officials and celebrities.

Revenue from the dinner finances journalism scholarships, and funds programming around press freedom. Sitting presidents typically attend, though President Trump was present at the dinner as president for the first time.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: What We Know So Far About the Shooting Suspect — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

  • The suspect was a guest of the Washington Hilton, where the White House Correspondents' Dinner was taking place.
  • The shooter is in custody and being evaluated at a hospital.
  • The gunman, who is from California, had a shotgun, a handgun and knives on him.
  • The suspect ran at a Secret Service security checkpoint.
  • The man shot one officer, who had a bulletproof vest on. President Trump, who was slated to speak at the event, said the officer was doing well.
  • The suspect is being charged with two counts-using a firearm during a crime or violence and assault on federal officers using a dangerous weapon.
  • The suspect's motivation and target remain unclear.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Lawmakers Condemn Shooting Incident — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

Lawmakers of both parties have condemned the shooting incident.

"Violence has NO place in our country," Rep. Steve Scalise (R., La.), a member of House leadership, who was present at the event, wrote on X.

"Praying for the safety of those who may remain in harm's way," House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D., N.Y.) wrote on X.

Speaker of the House Mike Johnson (R., La.), who was also at the event, was seen being evacuated by security staff.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Hilton Says It's Focused on Guests and Staff — WSJ
data.description:
By Craig Karmin

A spokesperson for Hilton directed questions to the Secret Service. In a statement, the hotel said Saturday night "our focus remains on the safety and wellbeing of our guests" and employees. According to the website, the massive and storied Washington Hilton has 1,107 rooms and 118,000 square feet of meeting and event space.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Suspect Was a Guest of Hotel Where Event Took Place — WSJ
data.description:
By Yoko Kubota

The suspect was a guest of the Washington Hilton, where the White House Correspondents' Dinner was taking place, said Washington D.C. interim Police Chief Jeffery Carroll.

The suspect has been transported to a local hospital where he is being evaluated, said Washington Mayor Muriel Bowser during a briefing.

The suspect is being charged with two counts-using a firearm during a crime or violence and assault on federal officers using a dangerous weapon, said Jeanine Pirro, the U.S. Attorney for the District of Columbia.

"It is clear, based upon what we know so far, that this individual was intent on doing as much harm and as much damage as he could," Pirro said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: After-Dinner Parties Continue as Planned in Washington — WSJ
data.description:
By Sabrina Rodriguez

A number of media outlets hosting after parties for the White House Correspondents' Dinner sent out notices that the parties were continuing as planned.

"We, along with our colleagues and friends across town this evening, will move forward with the event in recognition of the vital role journalists play everyday and especially at times like this," an email from TIME Magazine, which was hosting a party at the Swiss Ambassador's Residence, read.

MS NOW sent out an email saying "our doors are now open for those who are seeking a space to gather and have a bite to eat. While tonight's event won't be what we originally intended, we still think it is important to provide a space for friends and colleagues to be together."

As attendees were still being evacuated from the dinner, NBCUniversal sent out an email with the subject line "TAP (The After Party) Starts Now."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Dinner Guests Mid-Appetizer When Shooting Took Place — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

Guests were just finishing their spring pea & burrata salad appetizer, and wait staff were preparing to bring out the next course, when security yelled for everyone to get down. Silverware and cloth napkins fell to the ground as attendees ducked under tables.

As guests evacuated, staff cleared half-eaten plates of salad and baskets of leftover bread. Some attendees grabbed bottles and glasses of wine on their way out.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Suspected Shooter Had Multiple Guns and Knives — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

The suspected shooter at the White House Correspondents' Dinner had a shotgun, handgun and multiple knives, according to Washington D.C.'s Interim Police Chief, Jeffery Carroll.

The suspect ran at a U.S. Secret Service security checkpoint at about 8:36 p.m., Carroll said at a press conference Saturday night.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: 'When You're Impactful, They Go After You' — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

President Trump said "impactful" people are a target for violence.

"I want to live because I want to make this country great. That's why I want to live," Trump told reporters Saturday from the White House. "But when you're impactful, they go after you. When you're not impactful, they leave you alone."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Suspect's Target Still Unclear, Police Chief Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Joseph Pisani

Washington D.C.'s interim Police Chief, Jeffery Carroll said the target and motivation of the suspected shooter isn't yet known.

"We know he was running in the direction of the ballroom," Carroll said at a press conference Saturday night.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Capitol Police Confirm Security of U.S. Lawmakers — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

"The Members of Congress who attended the White House Correspondents' Dinner are safe and secure," Capitol Police wrote in an update on X.

"We are assisting our federal and local partners with anything they need," they said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: From Next-Generation Mobility to Global Expansion: iCAUR Demonstrated Its Vision at Beijing Auto Show 2026
data.description:
BEIJING, April 25, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- The Beijing Auto Show 2026, opened on April 24, marked a key moment for iCAUR as the brand presented two highlights: the ROBOX concept car and the right-hand-drive (RHD) V27, both presented by Dr. Su Jun, CEO of iCAUR.

The ROBOX concept car represents iCAUR’s exploration of future mobility. Named from a combination of “ROBOT” and “BOX,” it adopts a future-classic design philosophy, combining proportions with forward-looking design elements. It introduces a design language that merges a streamlined OneBox silhouette with a high-clearance structure, balancing aerodynamic efficiency with off-road capability. Distinctive features such as split round and rectangular lighting, clean modular surfaces, and a reimagined cabin space create a clear technological identity.

Alongside it, the right-hand-drive V27 reflects iCAUR’s ongoing global expansion. The left-hand-drive version of the V27, positioned as a new energy boxy vehicle with classic design, has gained strong market response in China and the Middle East. Its combination of distinctive styling and practical capability has contributed to its growing recognition among users in these markets.

Developed as a global model, the V27 meets five-star safety standards in China and Europe while adapting to right-hand-drive markets. Its introduction supports the brand’s expansion across Southeast Asia and Oceania, where diverse driving environments and user needs require localized solutions. The model reflects iCAUR’s ability to translate global standards into practical, market-relevant products.

Beyond its product lineup, iCAUR continues building a global user ecosystem. In partnership with China National Geographic, the brand is launching “The Grand Tour,” a global exploration program designed to connect users with diverse landscapes and cultural experiences.

At the same time, reflecting that each iCAUR is unique, the second season of the iCAUR Player Carnival invites users worldwide to participate in co-creation through vehicle customization projects, with selected works showcased at the SEMA Show. A range of customized versions of the V23 and V27 will also be displayed at the booth, highlighting the brand’s approach to personalization and user-driven design.

At the exhibition booth, the AIMOGA robot was featured as an interactive element, demonstrating iCAUR’s exploration of intelligent technologies. Through real-time interaction and scenario-based applications, it reflects the integration of mobility, AI, and user experience.

As iCAUR expands across more than 40 countries and regions, supported by a growing network of global partners, the Beijing Auto Show marks another step in the brand’s global development.

About iCAUR

iCAUR is a new-energy vehicle brand for global users, rooted in the philosophy that “Classic Never Fades.” By integrating design with advanced technology and global standards, iCAUR aims to become the first choice for users seeking a new-energy SUV with enduring character and versatile capability.

Contact Person:Zeng Zhaoqing

Email: cengzhaoqing@mychery.com

Website: https://www.icaurglobal.com/

Photos accompanying this announcement are available at

data.title: Venezuela's Interim President Condemns Shooting — WSJ
data.description:
By Sabrina Rodriguez

Venezuela's interim president Delcy Rodríguez condemned the shooting attempt at the White House Correspondents' Dinner.

"We condemn the attempted attack against the President @realDonaldTrump and his wife, Melania, to whom we extend our best wishes, as well as to the attendees of the Correspondents' Dinner," she wrote in a post in Spanish on X. "Violence will never be an option for those of us who uphold the banners of peace."

Rodríguez was installed as acting president in Caracas after a U.S. raid that captured the previous head of state, Nicolás Maduro, in January.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Promises New White House Correspondents' Dinner Soon — WSJ
data.description:
By Melissa Korn

President Trump said in a Truth Social post that the White House Correspondents' Association dinner would be rescheduled within 30 days. "I am ready, willing and able," he said at his press conference.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Incident Won't Deter Trump From 'Winning The War in Iran' — WSJ
data.description:
By Yoko Kubota

President Trump said that he doesn't believe the suspect was motivated by the Iran war, though he added that he doesn't know for certain. He suggested certain U.S. achievements could make him a target.

"It doesn't happen to people that don't do anything," he said at a press conference after the shooting. "It's not going to deter me from winning the war in Iran," he said.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Democrat Thanks Top Republican for Pulling to Secure Room — WSJ
data.description:
By WSJ Staff

Rep. Jared Moskowitz (D., Fla.) thanked law enforcement and top Republican lawmaker Steve Scalise (R., La.) "who grabbed me into a secure room." Scalise was nearly killed in a shooting at a baseball team practice in Alexandria, Va., in 2017.

The U.S. Capitol Police said the members of Congress who attended the White House Correspondents' Dinner are safe and secure.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Was Sitting Beside the First Lady, WHCA President at Dinner — WSJ
data.description:
By Anvee Bhutani

During the dinner, President Trump sat beside White House Correspondents' Association president Weijia Jiang and first landy Melania Trump.

The head table also included White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt, celebrity mentalist Oz Pearlman and officers from the WHCA board.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Trump Expresses Confidence in Secret Service — WSJ
data.description:
By Sabrina Rodriguez

President Trump praised the Secret Service for their response to the shooter's presence at the dinner. Asked if he was concerned about the Secret Service's protection ability, Trump said, "No, they acted very quickly." Trump said he was impressed by the Secret Service.

"You don't have to complain for me, I would be complaining. I'd be up here right now saying they didn't do their job," he added. "Oh, believe me, because, you know, it's my life, and I want to live because I want to make this country great."

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Suspect Likely 'Lone Wolf,' Was Far From Main Event, Trump Says — WSJ
data.description:
By Yoko Kubota

President Trump said he believes the suspect was likely acting on his own. "My impression is he was a lone wolf," he said.

Trump also said that the suspect was far from the room where the event was taking place, and the ballroom was very secure.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: LendingClub (LC) Q1 Earnings: What To Expect
data.description:
Digital lending platform LendingClub will be announcing earnings results this Monday afternoon. Here’s what to look for.

LendingClub beat analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $266.5 million, up 22.7% year on year. It was a strong quarter for the company, with and full-year EPS guidance beating analysts’ expectations.

Is LendingClub a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting LendingClub’s revenue to grow 14.4% year on year, slowing from the 20.5% increase it recorded in the same quarter last year.

Heading into earnings, analysts covering the company have grown increasingly bearish with revenue estimates seeing in majority downward revisions over the last 30 days. LendingClub has a history of exceeding Wall Street’s expectations.

Looking at LendingClub’s peers in the consumer finance segment, some have already reported their Q1 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. FirstCash delivered year-on-year revenue growth of 25.7%, beating analysts’ expectations by 4.8%, and Enova reported revenues up 17.4%, topping estimates by 2.8%. FirstCash traded up 3.3% following the results while Enova was down 1.6%.

Read our full analysis of FirstCash’s results here and Enova’s results here.

There has been positive sentiment among investors in the consumer finance segment, with share prices up 13.1% on average over the last month. LendingClub is up 26% during the same time and is heading into earnings with an average analyst price target of $22.50 (compared to the current share price of $17.46).

data.title: Earnings To Watch: FirstSun Capital Bancorp (FSUN) Reports Q1 Results Tomorrow

FirstSun Capital Bancorp missed analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $104 million, up 10.8% year on year. It was a slower quarter for the company, with a significant miss of analysts’ revenue estimates.

Is FirstSun Capital Bancorp a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.

This quarter, the market is expecting FirstSun Capital Bancorp’s revenue to grow 17.1% year on year, slowing from the 19.8% increase it recorded in the same quarter last year.

Analysts covering the company have generally reconfirmed their estimates over the last 30 days, suggesting they anticipate the business to stay the course heading into earnings. FirstSun Capital Bancorp has missed Wall Street’s revenue estimates multiple times over the last two years.

Looking at FirstSun Capital Bancorp’s peers in the regional banks segment, some have already reported their Q1 results, giving us a hint as to what we can expect. OFG Bancorp delivered year-on-year revenue growth of 4.2%, beating analysts’ expectations by 4.8%, and Republic Bancorp reported a revenue decline of 11.3%, falling short of estimates by 4.8%. OFG Bancorp traded up 7.6% following the results while Republic Bancorp was also up 1.4%.

data.title: Washington Hilton Was Scene of Attempted Reagan Assassination — WSJ
data.description:
By Tim Hanrahan

The Washington Hilton was the scene of the attempted assassination of President Ronald Reagan in 1981, who was shot and wounded by John Hinckley Jr. outside an entrance to the building after a speaking engagement at the hotel. That same entrance was used today for attendees lining up for the red carpet and others entering the White House Correspondents' Dinner event. The incident Saturday night was deeper into the hotel, inside the security perimeter but outside the large auditorium where President Trump sat at the head table.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.description:
Hardware products and merchandising solutions provider Hillman will be reporting results this Monday afternoon. Here’s what you need to know.

Hillman missed analysts’ revenue expectations last quarter, reporting revenues of $365.1 million, up 4.5% year on year. It was a slower quarter for the company, with a miss of analysts’ revenue estimates and full-year revenue guidance missing analysts’ expectations.

Is Hillman a buy or sell going into earnings? Read our full analysis here, it’s free for active Edge members.
